csharp.note: BUILD FAILURE — DiscoverAsync made private, 3 tests reference it

findings.issue: C# tests don't compile — DiscoverAsync was made private but 3 tests still call it (lines 305, 331, 346)
findings.impact: All 1500 C# tests cannot run. Runtime changes are completely unvalidated at the unit test level.
findings.suggestion: Update SetupTests to test through RunAsync (which calls DiscoverAsync internally) or make DiscoverAsync internal with InternalsVisibleTo

findings.issue: ErrorProps, ErrorInHandler, RecursionDepthLimit tests are broken — .pr files missing onError property. The coder hand-crafted some .pr files (ErrorCall works) but left these 3 unfixed.
findings.impact: These tests never worked — they were committed in a broken state. Error property access, error chain propagation, and recursion depth limiting have zero PLang test coverage.
findings.suggestion: Hand-craft the onError fields in the 3 broken .pr files, or document them as blocked and remove from test suite
findings.status: deferred — builder onError fix arriving soon, will unblock these tests

findings.issue: Test expects expression evaluation (e.g., '%x% > 5') but condition.if takes bool — Convert.ChangeType throws. Coder summary claims fix was applied but code still has 'public partial bool Condition'
findings.impact: Compound conditions are untested and don't work. NullReferenceException at runtime.
findings.suggestion: Either implement expression evaluation in condition.if (change Condition to string + add parser) or drop the test and document as unsupported syntax

findings.issue: Goal text says 'assert %result2% equals "content1"' (documenting known cache behavior) but .pr has Expected: 'content2'. Builder generated wrong assertion value.
findings.impact: Test was designed to document known cache behavior but doesn't match — assertion failure is a .pr configuration error, not a runtime bug
findings.suggestion: Fix the .pr assertion to match the goal intent ('content1' not 'content2')

findings.issue: Steps.RunAsync changed from 'return Data.Ok()' to 'return lastResult ?? Data.Ok()' — no C# test verifies this behavioral change. Deletion test: reverting to Data.Ok() would break no C# test.
findings.impact: Goal return value propagation is only tested by PLang integration tests. A regression would not be caught by C# unit tests.
findings.suggestion: Add C# test: create goal with steps, verify RunAsync returns last step's Data result (not Data.Ok())

findings.issue: Goals keying changed from Name to PrPath to prevent collisions. Get() now does linear scan via FirstOrDefault. Remove() has new fallback path. No C# test covers any of this.
findings.impact: Name collision prevention (the reason for the change) is completely untested. Performance regression from linear scan is undetected.
findings.suggestion: Add C# tests: (1) Add two goals with same Name but different PrPath, verify both accessible; (2) Remove by name when keyed by PrPath; (3) Get by name with linear scan

findings.issue: Setup discovery narrowed from scanning all .pr files to only 2 convention paths (.build/setup.pr and Setup/.build/setup.pr). No test verifies setup goals in non-standard locations are NOT discovered.
findings.impact: Low — this is a performance improvement with documented behavior change
findings.suggestion: Add C# test: place setup .pr in non-standard location, verify it's NOT discovered

findings.issue: Test runner removed post-run assertion error tracking — now returns raw goal result. TrackAssertionFailures AfterStep handler still exists but the backup check for assertion errors that bubble to goal level was removed.
findings.impact: Low — AfterStep handler catches most assertion failures. Edge case: assertion error in a step with on error ignore would be silently swallowed.
findings.suggestion: Acceptable tradeoff for cleaner code, but document the limitation

summary: C# tests don't compile (DiscoverAsync made private, 3 test references not updated). 5/64 PLang tests fail: 3 missing onError in .pr files (builder limitation, should have been hand-crafted), 1 NullRef in condition evaluation (claimed fix not applied), 1 wrong assertion value in .pr. Additionally, 5 runtime code changes lack C# test coverage — the behavioral changes to step/goal return values and goals keying are only validated by PLang integration tests.
